sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Listen to an example.

On the recording, you hear:

(narrator)
(man) That exam was just awful. 
(woman) Oh, it could have been worse. 
(narrator) What does the woman mean? 

In your test book, you read:
(A) The exam was really awful. 
(B) It was the worst exam she had ever seen. 
(C) It couldn't have been more difficult. 
(D) It wasn't that hard. 

You learn from the conversation that the man thought the exam was very difficult and that the woman disagreed with the man. The best answer to the question, "What does the woman mean?" is (D), "It wasn't that hard." Therefore, the correct choice is (D).

questions.passage:
W: Are you enjoying your coffee?
M: It tastes extremely bitter this morning!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The coffee is much better this morning.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the man says the coffee tastes 'extremely bitter,' which is the opposite of 'much better.'
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena pria itu mengatakan kopinya terasa 'sangat pahit', yang merupakan kebalikan dari 'jauh lebih baik'.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The man describes the coffee as 'extremely bitter,' which is a negative quality, not 'extremely good.'
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Pria itu menggambarkan kopi tersebut sebagai 'sangat pahit', yang merupakan kualitas negatif, bukan 'sangat enak'.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the man's statement 'It tastes extremely bitter' implies that the coffee is not good. Bitter is a negative taste in this context, and the word 'extremely' emphasizes this negative quality.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan pria itu 'Rasanya sangat pahit' menyiratkan bahwa kopi tersebut tidak enak. Pahit adalah rasa negatif dalam konteks ini, dan kata 'sangat' menekankan kualitas negatif ini.

options.text: This morning he definitely wants some coffee.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The dialogue is about the taste of the coffee, not about the man's desire for coffee this morning. His comment about bitterness does not indicate a definite want for it.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Dialog tersebut membahas tentang rasa kopi, bukan tentang keinginan pria itu untuk minum kopi pagi ini. Komentarnya tentang rasa pahit tidak menunjukkan keinginan yang pasti untuk meminumnya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 7 - Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
W: Can you tell me how often the philosophy class meets?
M: It meets twice a week, for an hour and a half each time.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The two classes meet in an hour and a half.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets the information. The dialogue states the class meets twice a week, for an hour and a half each time, not that two classes meet in total for that duration.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah menafsirkan informasi. Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu dua kali seminggu, selama satu setengah jam setiap kali, bukan bahwa dua kelas bertemu total selama durasi itu.

options.text: The class meets three hours per week.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, this is the correct answer. The dialogue states the class meets 'twice a week, for an hour and a half each time.' Multiplying these gives 2 * 1.5 hours = 3 hours per week. The answer paraphrases this calculation.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu 'dua kali seminggu, selama satu setengah jam setiap kali.' Mengalikan ini memberikan 2 * 1,5 jam = 3 jam per minggu. Jawaban tersebut memparafrasekan perhitungan ini.

options.text: Each half of the class is an hour long.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misunderstands the duration. The dialogue specifies 'an hour and a half' for each meeting, not that each 'half' of the class is an hour long.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah memahami durasi. Dialog menentukan 'satu setengah jam' untuk setiap pertemuan, bukan bahwa setiap 'setengah' dari kelas berdurasi satu jam.

options.text: Two times a week the class meets for an hour.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets the frequency and duration. The dialogue states the class meets twice a week for 1.5 hours each time, not that it meets for only one hour two times a week.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah menafsirkan frekuensi dan durasi. Dialog menyatakan kelas bertemu dua kali seminggu selama 1,5 jam setiap kali, bukan bahwa kelas hanya bertemu selama satu jam dua kali seminggu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
I'm tired of just sitting here!
M: W: Relax. I'm sure that the flight will depart within a few minutes.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: A few minutes ago, the flight departed.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman says the flight 'will depart within a few minutes,' implying it has not departed yet. The phrase 'a few minutes ago' indicates the past.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita itu mengatakan penerbangan 'akan berangkat dalam beberapa menit', menyiratkan bahwa penerbangan itu belum berangkat. Frasa 'beberapa menit yang lalu' menunjukkan masa lalu.

options.text: The fight will start in a while.

reason.en: This is a plausible paraphrase of 'depart within a few minutes,' but option [3] is a more direct and common synonym for the action of a flight leaving.
reason.id: Ini adalah parafrase yang masuk akal untuk 'berangkat dalam beberapa menit', tetapi opsi [3] adalah sinonim yang lebih langsung dan umum untuk tindakan keberangkatan pesawat.

options.text: They are frightened about the departure.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention fear or being frightened. The word 'tired' in the man's statement refers to impatience, not fear.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan rasa takut atau ketakutan. Kata 'lelah' dalam pernyataan pria itu mengacu pada ketidaksabaran, bukan ketakutan.

options.text: The plane is going to take off soon.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'the flight will depart within a few minutes' is best paraphrased by 'The plane is going to take off soon.' 'Depart' and 'take off' are synonyms in this context, and 'within a few minutes' is synonymous with 'soon.'
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita itu 'penerbangan akan berangkat dalam beberapa menit' paling baik diparafrasekan sebagai 'Pesawat akan lepas landas segera'. 'Berangkat' dan 'lepas landas' adalah sinonim dalam konteks ini, dan 'dalam beberapa menit' sinonim dengan 'segera'.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The science project is due next week.
M: I suppose I'll have to start working on it now.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He hasn't yet begun his project.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's statement 'I suppose I'll have to start working on it now' directly implies that he has not yet begun his project. This is the most logical inference from his statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, pernyataan pria 'I suppose I'll have to start working on it now' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia belum memulai proyeknya. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang paling logis dari pernyataannya.

options.text: He's supposed to do his science project next week.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the dialogue. The woman states the project is due next week, not that the man is supposed to do it next week. The man's response indicates he needs to start working on it because of the upcoming deadline.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan dialog. Wanita menyatakan proyek tersebut jatuh tempo minggu depan, bukan bahwa pria itu seharusnya mengerjakannya minggu depan. Respons pria itu menunjukkan bahwa dia perlu mulai mengerjakannya karena tenggat waktu yang akan datang.

options.text: He needs to start working on changing the due date.

reason.en: There is no mention or implication in the dialogue that the man needs to change the due date. His statement is about starting the work, not altering the deadline.
reason.id: Tidak ada penyebutan atau implikasi dalam dialog bahwa pria itu perlu mengubah tanggal jatuh tempo. Pernyataannya adalah tentang memulai pekerjaan, bukan mengubah tenggat waktu.

options.text: He's been working steadily on his science project.

reason.en: This contradicts the man's statement. If he had been working steadily, he would not feel the need to 'start working on it now' in response to the due date.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu. Jika dia telah bekerja terus menerus, dia tidak akan merasa perlu untuk 'mulai mengerjakannya sekarang' sebagai tanggapan atas tenggat waktu.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: I'd like to order a dozen roses. Do you deliver?
W: Yes. We can deliver anywhere in the city by this afternoon.
questions.question: 5. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSATION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

reason.en: The dialogue is about ordering flowers for delivery, not sending or receiving mail, so a post office is unlikely.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tentang memesan bunga untuk pengiriman, bukan mengirim atau menerima surat, jadi kantor pos tidak mungkin.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, the mention of ordering a 'dozen roses' and inquiring about 'delivery' strongly suggests the conversation is taking place in a florist shop.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, penyebutan pemesanan 'selusin mawar' dan pertanyaan tentang 'pengiriman' sangat menyarankan bahwa percakapan tersebut terjadi di toko bunga.

reason.en: The conversation is about flowers, not food service, making a restaurant an improbable location.
reason.id: Percakapan tersebut tentang bunga, bukan layanan makanan, menjadikan restoran sebagai lokasi yang tidak mungkin.

reason.en: A hospital delivery room is for childbirth, which is unrelated to ordering flowers.
reason.id: Ruang bersalin rumah sakit adalah untuk persalinan, yang tidak berhubungan dengan pemesanan bunga.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - SKILL 4: DRAW CONCLUSIONS ABOUT WHO, WHAT, WHERE

questions.passage:
W: Did you enjoy the biology lecture?
M: The professor droned on and on about cell division.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor drowned the cells in a lab.

reason.en: This is a distractor based on similar sounds. The word 'droned on' means to talk in a dull, monotonous way, not to physically drown something.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh berdasarkan bunyi yang mirip. Kata 'droned on' berarti berbicara dengan cara yang membosankan dan monoton, bukan menenggelamkan sesuatu secara fisik.

options.text: The lecture was long and boring.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'The professor droned on and on about cell division' implies that the lecture was lengthy and monotonous, which is synonymous with 'long and boring'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'The professor droned on and on about cell division' menyiratkan bahwa kuliah tersebut panjang dan monoton, yang sinonim dengan 'panjang dan membosankan'.

options.text: The professor divided the lecture into parts.

reason.en: This is a distractor. The phrase 'droned on and on' suggests a continuous, monotonous delivery, not a structured division of the lecture.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh. Frasa 'droned on and on' menunjukkan penyampaian yang terus-menerus dan monoton, bukan pembagian kuliah yang terstruktur.

options.text: The biologist tried to sell the results of the experiment.

reason.en: This is a distractor. The word 'droned' is misinterpreted as 'drove' or related to selling, and the context of a biology lecture is twisted to include selling experiment results.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh. Kata 'droned' disalahartikan sebagai 'drove' atau terkait dengan penjualan, dan konteks kuliah biologi diputarbalikkan untuk mencakup penjualan hasil eksperimen.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
M: What do I need to cash a check?
W: I have to see a driver's license and a credit card.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She needs to get a driver's license.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman states what *she* needs to see, not what she needs to get.
reason.id: Ini salah. Wanita itu menyatakan apa yang *dia* butuhkan untuk dilihat, bukan apa yang perlu dia dapatkan.

options.text: Two pieces of identification are necessary.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: DRAW CONCLUSIONS ABOUT WHO, WHAT, WHERE, the woman is stating the requirements for cashing a check. A driver's license and a credit card are two distinct forms of identification, implying that two are necessary.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: DRAW CONCLUSIONS ABOUT WHO, WHAT, WHERE, wanita itu menyatakan persyaratan untuk mencairkan cek. SIM dan kartu kredit adalah dua bentuk identifikasi yang berbeda, menyiratkan bahwa keduanya diperlukan.

options.text: The man should check to see if he needs credit.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman is stating her requirements, not advising the man on what he should check regarding credit.
reason.id: Ini salah. Wanita itu menyatakan persyaratannya, bukan menasihati pria itu tentang apa yang harus dia periksa terkait kredit.

options.text: A credit card can be used to get a driver's license.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue does not suggest any relationship between a credit card and obtaining a driver's license.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tersebut tidak menyarankan adanya hubungan antara kartu kredit dan mendapatkan SIM.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 4: DRAW CONCLUSIONS ABOUT WHO, WHAT, WHERE

questions.passage:
W: Have you been able to find an apartment yet?
M: It's difficult to find affordable housing in New York.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Housing within his budget is hard to locate.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'It's difficult to find affordable housing in New York' directly implies that finding housing within his budget is challenging. Option [0] accurately paraphrases this meaning.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'It's difficult to find affordable housing in New York' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa menemukan perumahan yang sesuai dengan anggarannya sulit. Pilihan [0] secara akurat memparafrasekan makna ini.

options.text: It's hard to find his house in New York.

reason.en: This option misinterprets 'housing' as 'house' and suggests the man is lost, which is not implied by the dialogue. The dialogue is about the difficulty of finding a place to live, not about being unable to locate his current residence.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan 'housing' sebagai 'house' dan menyarankan bahwa pria itu tersesat, yang tidak tersirat dalam dialog. Dialog ini tentang kesulitan menemukan tempat tinggal, bukan tentang ketidakmampuan menemukan kediamannya saat ini.

options.text: He can't afford to move his house to New York.

reason.en: This option introduces the idea of 'moving his house,' which is not mentioned or implied. The dialogue focuses on the difficulty of finding affordable housing, not the logistics of moving a physical structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini memperkenalkan gagasan 'memindahkan rumahnya', yang tidak disebutkan atau tersirat. Dialog ini berfokus pada kesulitan menemukan perumahan yang terjangkau, bukan logistik memindahkan struktur fisik.

options.text: Housing in New York is unavailable.

reason.en: The man states it is 'difficult to find affordable housing,' not that housing is 'unavailable.' This implies a challenge in finding something within a certain price range, not a complete lack of options.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa 'sulit menemukan perumahan yang terjangkau', bukan bahwa perumahan 'tidak tersedia'. Ini menyiratkan tantangan dalam menemukan sesuatu dalam kisaran harga tertentu, bukan ketiadaan pilihan sama sekali.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Why were you so late in getting home from work?
M: My boss had me finish all the month-end reports.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The boss was working on the reports.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the dialogue states 'My boss had me finish...', indicating the man was the one doing the work, not the boss.
reason.id: Ini salah karena dialog menyatakan 'Bos saya meminta saya menyelesaikan...', yang menunjukkan bahwa pria itu yang melakukan pekerjaan, bukan bosnya.

options.text: He would have to finish the reports before the end of next month.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The phrase 'month-end reports' refers to reports due at the end of the current month, not the next month. The man was late because he had to finish them.
reason.id: Ini salah. Frasa 'laporan akhir bulan' mengacu pada laporan yang jatuh tempo di akhir bulan berjalan, bukan bulan depan. Pria itu terlambat karena dia harus menyelesaikannya.

options.text: He was directed to stay late and finish some work.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, the phrase 'My boss had me finish all the month-end reports' implies that the boss instructed or directed the man to stay late to complete the reports. This is the most logical inference.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, frasa 'Bos saya meminta saya menyelesaikan semua laporan akhir bulan' menyiratkan bahwa bos menginstruksikan atau mengarahkan pria itu untuk lembur untuk menyelesaikan laporan tersebut. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang paling logis.

options.text: He could finish the reports at home.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue implies the work was done at the office because he was late getting home from work. There is no mention of taking the reports home.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog menyiratkan bahwa pekerjaan dilakukan di kantor karena dia terlambat pulang kerja. Tidak ada penyebutan tentang membawa laporan ke rumah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 4 - Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
M: Ms. Jones did not look too happy as she left her classroom.
W: She was angered by her rowdy students.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The boisterous students made the teacher mad.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'She was angered by her rowdy students' is a paraphrase of the man's observation that 'Ms. Jones did not look too happy as she left her classroom.' 'Rowdy' is a synonym for 'boisterous,' and 'angered' is a synonym for 'mad.' The meaning is preserved, and synonyms are used.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'She was angered by her rowdy students' adalah parafrasa dari pengamatan pria bahwa 'Ms. Jones did not look too happy as she left her classroom.' 'Rowdy' adalah sinonim untuk 'boisterous,' dan 'angered' adalah sinonim untuk 'mad.' Maknanya terjaga, dan sinonim digunakan.

options.text: The teacher angered the students with the exam results.

reason.en: This option reverses the cause and effect. The dialogue states the teacher was angered by the students, not the other way around.
reason.id: Opsi ini membalikkan sebab dan akibat. Dialog menyatakan bahwa guru marah karena siswa, bukan sebaliknya.

options.text: The students were angry that the teacher was around.

reason.en: This option incorrectly assumes the students were angry and that the teacher's presence was the cause. The dialogue clearly indicates the teacher was unhappy due to the students' behavior.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru berasumsi bahwa siswa marah dan kehadiran guru adalah penyebabnya. Dialog dengan jelas menunjukkan bahwa guru tidak senang karena perilaku siswa.

options.text: The angered students complained to the teacher.

reason.en: This option suggests the students complained to the teacher, which is not mentioned in the dialogue. The dialogue focuses on the teacher's reaction to the students' behavior.
reason.id: Opsi ini menyarankan bahwa siswa mengeluh kepada guru, yang tidak disebutkan dalam dialog. Dialog berfokus pada reaksi guru terhadap perilaku siswa.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The prices at this store are really outrageous!
M: You can say that again!
questions.question: 11. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: The man's statement 'You can say that again!' is an idiom that expresses strong agreement, not disagreement. The word 'outrageous' means excessively expensive, so 'reasonable' is the opposite.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom yang mengekspresikan persetujuan kuat, bukan ketidaksetujuan. Kata 'outrageous' berarti sangat mahal, sehingga 'reasonable' adalah kebalikannya.

options.text: The store is too far out of town.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the prices, not the location of the store. The phrase 'You can say that again!' is an expression of agreement.
reason.id: Percakapan ini tentang harga, bukan lokasi toko. Frasa 'You can say that again!' adalah ekspresi persetujuan.

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: The phrase 'You can say that again!' is an idiom for agreement, not a request for repetition. The speaker is emphasizing their agreement with the woman's statement.
reason.id: Frasa 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom untuk persetujuan, bukan permintaan untuk mengulang. Pembicara menekankan persetujuannya dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, the man's response 'You can say that again!' is a common idiom used to strongly agree with what the previous speaker said. The woman stated the prices are 'outrageous' (very high), and the man is confirming this.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement, respons pria 'You can say that again!' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk sangat setuju dengan apa yang dikatakan pembicara sebelumnya. Wanita itu menyatakan bahwa harga 'outrageous' (sangat tinggi), dan pria itu mengkonfirmasi hal ini.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
W: I don't like this weather very much.
M: We haven't see rain like this for many years!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: It's rained unusually hard this year.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the man's statement 'We haven't see rain like this for many years!' implies that the current amount of rain is unusual and significant, meaning it has rained unusually hard this year. This is a correct interpretation of the negative statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan pria 'We haven't see rain like this for many years!' menyiratkan bahwa jumlah hujan saat ini tidak biasa dan signifikan, yang berarti tahun ini hujannya sangat deras. Ini adalah interpretasi yang benar dari pernyataan negatif tersebut.

options.text: There hasn't been any rain for many years.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the man's statement implies that there HAS been rain, but not like this for many years. It directly contradicts the idea that there hasn't been any rain.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena pernyataan pria menyiratkan bahwa memang ADA hujan, tetapi tidak seperti ini selama bertahun-tahun. Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa belum ada hujan sama sekali.

options.text: It's been many years since it rained.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The man's statement 'We haven't see rain like this for many years!' means that the current rain is exceptional, not that it has been many years since it rained at all. The implication is that rain has occurred, but not of this intensity.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pernyataan pria 'We haven't see rain like this for many years!' berarti bahwa hujan saat ini luar biasa, bukan bahwa sudah bertahun-tahun sejak hujan turun sama sekali. Implikasinya adalah hujan telah terjadi, tetapi tidak dengan intensitas seperti ini.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The man's statement focuses on the unusual intensity of the rain, not his personal feelings about rain. While he might not like it, his statement is about the weather event itself.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Pernyataan pria berfokus pada intensitas hujan yang tidak biasa, bukan pada perasaan pribadinya tentang hujan. Meskipun dia mungkin tidak menyukainya, pernyataannya adalah tentang peristiwa cuaca itu sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions)

questions.passage:
M: Professor Martin, what do you think of the composition that I turned in last week?
W: Without question, you need to improve the quality of your writing.
questions.question: 13. WHAT DOES PROFESSOR MARTIN SAY ABOUT THE STUDENT?

options.text: He needs to do a better job writing questions.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets 'composition' as 'questions' and 'writing' as 'writing questions'. The professor is commenting on the student's writing quality, not their ability to formulate questions.

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to a welcome address by a member of a club. 
(woman) Welcome to this introductory meeting for new members of the Sierra Club. The Sierra Club is an organization whose goals are centered on the protection of the environment. It was founded in 1892 in San Francisco by naturalist John Muir, who was intent on preserving the natural beauty and harmony of the Sierra Nevadas in eastern California. 

Today the Sierra Club boasts almost 200,000 members in all fifty states of the United States. Through activities such as conferences, lectures, exhibits, and films, the organization works to continue the effort begun by John Muir. The Sierra Club also publishes a weekly newsletter, a bimonthly magazine, and various books.

Although they do have several similarities, realism and naturalism should be clearly differentiated. The realist objectively reports on events, with the accuracy of the description as the prime motive. The naturalist, on the other hand, has more of a philosophic bent; naturalist writings express the writer's philosophy that human actions are determined by natural laws such as heredity and environment.
groups.label: Q 47-50

sections.instruction:
Time—approximately 35 minutes 
(including the reading of the directions for each part) 

In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.

sections.title: STRUCTURE AND WRITTEN EXPRESSION

parts.title: Structure
parts.instruction:
This section is designed to measure your ability to recognize language that is appropriate for standard written English. There are two types of questions in this section, with special directions for each type.

Questions 1-15 are incomplete sentences. Beneath each sentence you will see four words or phrases, marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Choose the one word or phrase that best completes the sentence. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. Fill in the space so that the letter inside the oval cannot be seen. 

Look at the following examples 
The president ____________ the election by a landslide.
(A) won 
(B) he won 
(C) yesterday 
(D) fortunately 

The sentence should read, "The president won the election by a landslide." Therefore, you should choose (A).

questions.question: The North Pole ___________ a latitude of 90 degrees north.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because 'The North Pole' is the subject and 'it has' introduces another subject and verb without proper conjunction or punctuation. The sentence already has a subject ('The North Pole'), so adding 'it' is redundant and grammatically incorrect.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang terlalu panjang (run-on sentence) karena 'The North Pole' adalah subjek dan 'it has' memperkenalkan subjek dan kata kerja lain tanpa konjungsi atau tanda baca yang tepat. Kalimat sudah memiliki subjek ('The North Pole'), sehingga menambahkan 'it' berlebihan dan secara tata bahasa tidak benar.

reason.en: The verb 'is having' is in the present continuous tense, which is typically used for actions happening at the moment of speaking or for temporary situations. The latitude of the North Pole is a permanent fact, so the present continuous is inappropriate here. The sentence needs a simple present tense verb.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'is having' dalam bentuk present continuous tense, yang biasanya digunakan untuk tindakan yang terjadi saat berbicara atau situasi sementara. Garis lintang Kutub Utara adalah fakta permanen, sehingga present continuous tidak tepat di sini. Kalimat membutuhkan kata kerja dalam bentuk simple present tense.

reason.en: This option uses a relative clause ('which is having') but it does not form a complete sentence. 'The North Pole' is the subject, and 'which is having...' acts as a dependent clause. A complete sentence requires an independent clause, meaning a main verb is needed to complete the thought about the North Pole.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan klausa relatif ('which is having') tetapi tidak membentuk kalimat yang lengkap. 'The North Pole' adalah subjek, dan 'which is having...' berfungsi sebagai klausa dependen. Kalimat lengkap membutuhkan klausa independen, yang berarti kata kerja utama diperlukan untuk melengkapi gagasan tentang Kutub Utara.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb, this option correctly provides the main verb 'has' to complete the sentence. 'The North Pole' is the subject, and 'has' is the simple present tense verb that agrees with the singular subject, forming a complete and grammatically correct sentence.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb, opsi ini dengan benar menyediakan kata kerja utama 'has' untuk melengkapi kalimat. 'The North Pole' adalah subjek, dan 'has' adalah kata kerja simple present tense yang sesuai dengan subjek tunggal, membentuk kalimat yang lengkap dan benar secara tata bahasa.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The city of Beverly Hills is surrounded on ___________ the city of Los Angeles.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect. 'Its sides' is a possessive noun phrase and cannot function as the object of the preposition 'on' in this context. The sentence requires a prepositional phrase that indicates location or manner.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara tata bahasa tidak benar. 'Its sides' adalah frasa kata benda posesif dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai objek dari preposisi 'on' dalam konteks ini. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan frasa preposisional yang menunjukkan lokasi atau cara.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence or a grammatically awkward construction. 'The sides are' is a clause that cannot directly follow the preposition 'on' without proper conjunction or restructuring.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang terlalu panjang (run-on) atau konstruksi yang canggung secara tata bahasa. 'The sides are' adalah klausa yang tidak dapat mengikuti preposisi 'on' secara langsung tanpa konjungsi atau restrukturisasi yang tepat.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect and does not fit the intended meaning. 'It is the side of' is a clause that cannot be used to complete the prepositional phrase starting with 'on'.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara tata bahasa tidak benar dan tidak sesuai dengan makna yang dimaksud. 'It is the side of' adalah klausa yang tidak dapat digunakan untuk melengkapi frasa preposisional yang dimulai dengan 'on'.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 56: Prepositions, the phrase 'on all sides by' is a common idiomatic expression used to describe something being surrounded. 'All sides' functions correctly as part of the prepositional phrase indicating the extent of the surrounding.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 56: Prepositions, frasa 'on all sides by' adalah ekspresi idiomatik umum yang digunakan untuk menggambarkan sesuatu yang dikelilingi. 'All sides' berfungsi dengan benar sebagai bagian dari frasa preposisional yang menunjukkan sejauh mana pengepungan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 56: Prepositions

questions.question: ___________ greyhound, can achieve speeds up to thirty-six miles per hour.

reason.en: This option provides only the article 'The,' which is insufficient to form a complete and grammatically correct sentence. The sentence requires a subject and a predicate, and 'The' alone does not fulfill this.
reason.id: Opsi ini hanya menyediakan artikel 'The,' yang tidak cukup untuk membentuk kalimat yang lengkap dan benar secara tata bahasa. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan subjek dan predikat, dan 'The' saja tidak memenuhi hal ini.

reason.en: While 'The fastest' correctly uses the superlative form, it still lacks a noun to act as the subject of the sentence. The sentence needs to specify *what* is the fastest.
reason.id: Meskipun 'The fastest' menggunakan bentuk superlatif dengan benar, opsi ini masih kekurangan kata benda untuk bertindak sebagai subjek kalimat. Kalimat tersebut perlu menentukan *apa* yang tercepat.

reason.en: This option provides 'The fastest dog,' which correctly identifies the subject. However, it is missing the appositive phrase 'the greyhound' which is essential for providing specific information about the dog, as indicated by the comma after 'greyhound' in the original sentence structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini menyediakan 'The fastest dog,' yang mengidentifikasi subjek dengan benar. Namun, opsi ini kehilangan frasa aposisi 'the greyhound' yang penting untuk memberikan informasi spesifik tentang anjing tersebut, sebagaimana ditunjukkan oleh koma setelah 'greyhound' dalam struktur kalimat aslinya.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives, and the need for a complete subject and appositive phrase, 'The fastest dog, the' correctly sets up the sentence. 'The fastest dog' acts as the subject, and 'the greyhound' (implied to follow the comma) serves as an appositive, renaming the subject. The structure 'The [superlative] [noun], the [noun]' is a common way to introduce a specific example of a superlative category.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives, dan kebutuhan akan subjek yang lengkap serta frasa aposisi, 'The fastest dog, the' secara tepat menyiapkan kalimat. 'The fastest dog' bertindak sebagai subjek, dan 'the greyhound' (tersirat mengikuti koma) berfungsi sebagai aposisi, yang menamai ulang subjek. Struktur 'The [superlatif] [kata benda], the [kata benda]' adalah cara umum untuk memperkenalkan contoh spesifik dari kategori superlatif.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives

questions.question: Marmots spend their time foraging among meadow plants and flowers or__________ on rocky cliffs.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the sentence structure requires a verb in the present participle form to parallel 'foraging'. 'Gets sun' is not parallel to 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena struktur kalimat membutuhkan kata kerja dalam bentuk present participle agar sejajar dengan 'foraging'. 'Gets sun' tidak sejajar dengan 'foraging'.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 30-32 (Form of the Verb), this option is correct because 'sunning' is the present participle form that correctly parallels the gerund 'foraging' in the sentence, creating a parallel structure.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 30-32 (Form of the Verb), opsi ini benar karena 'sunning' adalah bentuk present participle yang secara tepat sejajar dengan gerund 'foraging' dalam kalimat, menciptakan struktur paralel.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'the sun' is a noun phrase and does not fit grammatically or logically to parallel the action of 'foraging'. The sentence needs a verb form here.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'the sun' adalah frasa kata benda dan tidak cocok secara tata bahasa atau logika untuk menyejajarkan tindakan 'foraging'. Kalimat membutuhkan bentuk kata kerja di sini.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'sunny' is an adjective. The sentence requires a verb form to parallel 'foraging', not an adjective.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'sunny' adalah kata sifat. Kalimat membutuhkan bentuk kata kerja untuk menyejajarkan 'foraging', bukan kata sifat.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 30–32 — Form of the Verb

questions.question: The greenhouse effect occurs _______________ heat radiated from the Sun.

options.text: when does the Earth s atmosphere trap

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment because 'when does the Earth's atmosphere trap' is a question clause and cannot stand alone as a complete sentence following the initial clause 'The greenhouse effect occurs'.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat karena 'when does the Earth's atmosphere trap' adalah klausa pertanyaan dan tidak dapat berdiri sendiri sebagai kalimat lengkap setelah klausa awal 'The greenhouse effect occurs'.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment. 'does the Earth's atmosphere trap' is an inverted question structure and cannot function as the main clause or a subordinate clause in this context.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat. 'does the Earth's atmosphere trap' adalah struktur pertanyaan terbalik dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai klausa utama atau klausa subordinat dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 6: Connect Multiple Clauses Correctly, this option correctly forms a complex sentence. 'The greenhouse effect occurs' is the independent clause, and 'when the Earth's atmosphere traps heat radiated from the Sun' is a subordinate adverbial clause introduced by the subordinating conjunction 'when', correctly completing the sentence.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 6: Connect Multiple Clauses Correctly, opsi ini secara benar membentuk kalimat kompleks. 'The greenhouse effect occurs' adalah klausa independen, dan 'when the Earth's atmosphere traps heat radiated from the Sun' adalah klausa adverbial subordinat yang diawali oleh konjungsi subordinatif 'when', yang melengkapi kalimat dengan benar.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence or a comma splice if a comma were implied before 'the Earth's atmosphere'. It attempts to join two independent clauses ('The greenhouse effect occurs' and 'the Earth's atmosphere traps...') without a proper connector or punctuation.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang terlalu panjang (run-on sentence) atau comma splice jika koma tersirat sebelum 'the Earth's atmosphere'. Opsi ini mencoba menggabungkan dua klausa independen ('The greenhouse effect occurs' dan 'the Earth's atmosphere traps...') tanpa penghubung atau tanda baca yang tepat.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION: Skills 6–8 — Connect Multiple Clauses Correctly

questions.question: The Rose Bowl, ________ place on New Year's Day, is the oldest postseason collegiate football game in the United States.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because 'The Rose Bowl' is the subject, and 'takes' would be the verb. However, the sentence already has a verb ('is') for the main subject. Adding another main verb without a connector or relative pronoun is incorrect.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang berlebihan (run-on sentence) karena 'The Rose Bowl' adalah subjek, dan 'takes' akan menjadi kata kerja. Namun, kalimat tersebut sudah memiliki kata kerja ('is') untuk subjek utama. Menambahkan kata kerja utama lain tanpa konektor atau kata ganti relatif adalah salah.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence with two independent clauses ('The Rose Bowl it takes place...' and '...is the oldest...') joined incorrectly without a proper conjunction or punctuation. The pronoun 'it' is redundant here.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat dengan dua klausa independen ('The Rose Bowl it takes place...' dan '...is the oldest...') yang digabungkan secara tidak benar tanpa konjungsi atau tanda baca yang tepat. Kata ganti 'it' di sini berlebihan.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 13: Reduced Clauses, this option correctly uses a reduced relative clause. 'which takes place on New Year's Day' modifies 'The Rose Bowl'. The full clause would be 'The Rose Bowl, which takes place on New Year's Day, is...'. This is a common structure in TOEFL.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 13: Reduced Clauses, opsi ini dengan benar menggunakan klausa relatif yang dikurangi. 'which takes place on New Year's Day' memodifikasi 'The Rose Bowl'. Klausa lengkapnya adalah 'The Rose Bowl, which takes place on New Year's Day, is...'. Ini adalah struktur yang umum dalam TOEFL.

reason.en: This option uses the past tense verb 'took'. While the Rose Bowl is an event that happened in the past, the sentence structure requires a clause that describes the Rose Bowl in relation to its current status ('is the oldest'). Using 'took' here without a relative pronoun would create a sentence fragment or an incorrect structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata kerja lampau 'took'. Meskipun Rose Bowl adalah acara yang terjadi di masa lalu, struktur kalimat memerlukan klausa yang menggambarkan Rose Bowl dalam kaitannya dengan statusnya saat ini ('is the oldest'). Menggunakan 'took' di sini tanpa kata ganti relatif akan menciptakan fragmen kalimat atau struktur yang salah.

questions.tested_skill: Structure Questions (Skills 1–19) - Skill 13: Reduced Clauses

questions.question: Experiments ________ represent a giant step into the medicine of the future.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The sentence requires a subject and a verb. 'Experiments' is the subject. The phrase 'using gene therapy' functions as a reduced relative clause modifying 'Experiments', effectively meaning 'Experiments that use gene therapy'. This structure correctly completes the sentence.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan subjek dan kata kerja. 'Experiments' adalah subjeknya. Frasa 'using gene therapy' berfungsi sebagai klausa relatif yang direduksi (reduced relative clause) yang memodifikasi 'Experiments', secara efektif berarti 'Experiments that use gene therapy'. Struktur ini melengkapi kalimat dengan benar.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a run-on sentence. 'Experiments' is the subject, and 'use gene therapy' is a verb phrase. Adding this directly after 'Experiments' would result in a sentence with two main verbs for the same subject without proper conjunction or punctuation, or it would create a sentence fragment if 'Experiments' were intended as part of a larger structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menciptakan kalimat yang terlalu panjang (run-on sentence). 'Experiments' adalah subjek, dan 'use gene therapy' adalah frasa kata kerja. Menambahkan ini langsung setelah 'Experiments' akan menghasilkan kalimat dengan dua kata kerja utama untuk subjek yang sama tanpa konjungsi atau tanda baca yang tepat, atau akan menciptakan fragmen kalimat jika 'Experiments' dimaksudkan sebagai bagian dari struktur yang lebih besar.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. 'They use' is a clause that needs to be connected to the main subject ('Experiments') with a subordinating conjunction (e.g., 'because,' 'when') or a relative pronoun. As it stands, it creates a sentence fragment.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. 'They use' adalah klausa yang perlu dihubungkan ke subjek utama ('Experiments') dengan konjungsi subordinatif (misalnya, 'because,' 'when') atau kata ganti relatif. Dalam bentuknya yang sekarang, ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. 'Gene therapy uses' is a subject-verb phrase. If placed after 'Experiments', it would create a sentence with two subjects ('Experiments' and 'gene therapy') that are not properly connected or related in a way that makes grammatical sense in this context. It would imply 'Experiments' is the subject and 'gene therapy uses' is another subject, which is illogical.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. 'Gene therapy uses' adalah frasa subjek-kata kerja. Jika ditempatkan setelah 'Experiments', ini akan menciptakan kalimat dengan dua subjek ('Experiments' dan 'gene therapy') yang tidak terhubung dengan benar atau tidak berhubungan sedemikian rupa sehingga masuk akal secara tata bahasa dalam konteks ini. Ini akan menyiratkan bahwa 'Experiments' adalah subjek dan 'gene therapy uses' adalah subjek lain, yang tidak logis.

questions.tested_skill: Structure Questions (Skills 1-19) - Skill 13: Reduced Clauses

questions.question: ______________ off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead.

options.text: While some types of coral reefs

reason.en: This option correctly uses a subordinate clause ('While some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living') to contrast with the independent clause ('others are dead'). The word 'While' functions as a subordinating conjunction, introducing a dependent clause that sets up a contrast.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara tepat menggunakan klausa subordinat ('While some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living') untuk kontras dengan klausa independen ('others are dead'). Kata 'While' berfungsi sebagai konjungsi subordinatif, memperkenalkan klausa dependen yang menyiapkan kontras.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence. 'Some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' is an independent clause, and 'others are dead' is also an independent clause. They cannot be joined simply by placing them next to each other without a connector or proper punctuation.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang berlarut-larut (run-on sentence). 'Some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' adalah klausa independen, dan 'others are dead' juga merupakan klausa independen. Keduanya tidak dapat digabungkan begitu saja tanpa konektor atau tanda baca yang tepat.

options.text: There are many types of coral reefs

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment. 'There are many types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' is grammatically incorrect and does not form a complete thought. It attempts to combine a statement about the existence of many types with a statement about their living status in an awkward way.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat. 'There are many types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' secara tata bahasa tidak benar dan tidak membentuk pemikiran yang lengkap. Opsi ini mencoba menggabungkan pernyataan tentang keberadaan banyak jenis dengan pernyataan tentang status hidup mereka dengan cara yang canggung.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment. 'Coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' is a complete clause, but 'others are dead' is a separate idea that is not properly connected. It lacks a subordinate clause or a coordinating conjunction to link the two contrasting ideas.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat. 'Coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living' adalah klausa yang lengkap, tetapi 'others are dead' adalah ide terpisah yang tidak terhubung dengan benar. Opsi ini kekurangan klausa subordinat atau konjungsi koordinatif untuk menghubungkan kedua ide yang kontras.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 6: Connect Multiple Clauses Correctly

questions.question: Nimbostratus clouds are thick, dark gray clouds ____________ forebode rain.

reason.en: Option [0] 'what' is incorrect because 'what' is used to introduce noun clauses or as a determiner/pronoun, but here we need a relative pronoun to introduce an adjective clause modifying 'clouds'.
reason.id: Opsi [0] 'what' salah karena 'what' digunakan untuk memperkenalkan klausa nomina atau sebagai determinator/pronomina, tetapi di sini kita memerlukan kata ganti relatif untuk memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva yang memodifikasi 'clouds'.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 9: Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors, option [1] 'which' is the correct relative pronoun to introduce an adjective clause modifying the noun 'clouds'. The clause 'which forebode rain' describes the clouds.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 9: Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors, opsi [1] 'which' adalah kata ganti relatif yang benar untuk memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva yang memodifikasi kata benda 'clouds'. Klausa 'which forebode rain' menjelaskan awan tersebut.

reason.en: Option [2] 'what they' is incorrect because 'what' cannot function as a relative pronoun in this context, and 'they' is redundant as the subject of the adjective clause is implied by the relative pronoun.
reason.id: Opsi [2] 'what they' salah karena 'what' tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai kata ganti relatif dalam konteks ini, dan 'they' berlebihan karena subjek klausa adjektiva tersirat oleh kata ganti relatif.

reason.en: Option [3] 'which they' is incorrect because while 'which' is a correct relative pronoun, the addition of 'they' creates a redundant subject. The relative pronoun 'which' already serves as the subject of the adjective clause.
reason.id: Opsi [3] 'which they' salah karena meskipun 'which' adalah kata ganti relatif yang benar, penambahan 'they' menciptakan subjek yang berlebihan. Kata ganti relatif 'which' sudah berfungsi sebagai subjek klausa adjektiva.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION, Skills 9–12 — Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors

questions.question: Some economists now suggest that home equity loans are merely a new trap to push consumers beyond ______

reason.en: This option is grammatically incomplete. The phrase 'push consumers beyond they can afford' lacks a proper conjunction or relative pronoun to connect 'consumers' with the clause 'they can afford'.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak lengkap secara tata bahasa. Frasa 'push consumers beyond they can afford' kekurangan konjungsi atau kata ganti relatif yang tepat untuk menghubungkan 'consumers' dengan klausa 'they can afford'.

reason.en: This option is grammatically awkward and redundant. 'Push consumers beyond they can afford it' uses 'it' unnecessarily, and the structure 'beyond they can afford it' is not standard English.
reason.id: Opsi ini canggung secara tata bahasa dan berlebihan. 'Push consumers beyond they can afford it' menggunakan 'it' secara tidak perlu, dan struktur 'beyond they can afford it' bukan bahasa Inggris standar.

reason.en: Based on Reading Skill 11: Use Context for Simple Words, this option correctly completes the sentence by using 'what is affordable' to refer to the level of affordability. The phrase 'push consumers beyond what is affordable' logically means pushing them beyond the point where they can afford something, fitting the context of economists suggesting a new trap.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Reading Skill 11: Use Context for Simple Words, opsi ini melengkapi kalimat dengan benar dengan menggunakan 'what is affordable' untuk merujuk pada tingkat keterjangkauan. Frasa 'push consumers beyond what is affordable' secara logis berarti mendorong mereka melampaui titik di mana mereka mampu membeli sesuatu, sesuai dengan konteks para ekonom yang menyarankan jebakan baru.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect in this context. 'Able to afford' is an adjective phrase and cannot directly follow 'beyond' to complete the meaning of the sentence. It requires a noun or a noun clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara tata bahasa salah dalam konteks ini. 'Able to afford' adalah frasa kata sifat dan tidak dapat langsung mengikuti 'beyond' untuk melengkapi makna kalimat. Ini membutuhkan kata benda atau klausa kata benda.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION THREE — READING COMPREHENSION, Skill 11 — Use Context for Simple Words

questions.question: People who reverse the letters of words __________ to read suffer from dyslexia.

reason.en: This is the correct answer because 'when trying' is a reduced clause. The full clause would be 'when they are trying.' This structure is common in English and fits grammatically into the sentence.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena 'when trying' adalah klausa yang direduksi. Klausa lengkapnya adalah 'when they are trying.' Struktur ini umum dalam bahasa Inggris dan cocok secara gramatikal ke dalam kalimat.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'if they tried' introduces a conditional clause that implies a hypothetical situation. The sentence describes a characteristic of people with dyslexia, not a hypothetical condition for reading.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'if they tried' memperkenalkan klausa kondisional yang menyiratkan situasi hipotetis. Kalimat tersebut menggambarkan karakteristik orang dengan disleksia, bukan kondisi hipotetis untuk membaca.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. 'When tried' is a passive reduced clause. The sentence requires an active participle phrase to describe the action the people are performing ('trying to read'), not an action being done to them.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. 'When tried' adalah klausa pasif yang direduksi. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan frasa partisip aktif untuk menggambarkan tindakan yang dilakukan orang ('mencoba membaca'), bukan tindakan yang dilakukan pada mereka.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'if he tries' uses a singular pronoun ('he') and a present tense verb, which does not agree with the plural subject 'People' and the context of describing a general characteristic.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'if he tries' menggunakan kata ganti tunggal ('he') dan kata kerja bentuk sekarang, yang tidak sesuai dengan subjek jamak 'People' dan konteks menggambarkan karakteristik umum.

questions.tested_skill: Structure Questions (Skills 1–19) - Skill 13: Reduced Clauses

questions.question: Featured at the Henry Ford Museum _______ of antique cars dating from 1865.

reason.en: This is the correct answer because the sentence needs both a subject ('an exhibit') and a verb ('is') to be complete. reason.id: Kata 'flat-topped' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi kata benda 'mountains'. Ini adalah bagian dari frasa subjek dan bukan kesalahannya.

reason.en: Option (A) 'weighing' is a present participle. While participles can be part of verb phrases, they cannot stand alone as the main verb of a sentence without a helping verb. The sentence structure here requires a main verb.
reason.id: Opsi (A) 'weighing' adalah present participle. Meskipun participle bisa menjadi bagian dari frasa kata kerja, mereka tidak bisa berdiri sendiri sebagai kata kerja utama kalimat tanpa kata kerja bantu. Struktur kalimat di sini membutuhkan kata kerja utama.

reason.en: Option (B) 'as' is a preposition or conjunction. It is not a verb and cannot function as the main verb of the sentence. It is often used in comparisons, but the sentence requires a verb to complete the thought about the sapphires.

reason.en: Option (C) 'two pounds' is a noun phrase indicating weight. It is not a verb and cannot serve as the main verb of the sentence. The sentence needs an action or state of being to describe what the sapphires do or are.
reason.id: Opsi (C) 'two pounds' adalah frasa kata benda yang menunjukkan berat. Ini bukan kata kerja dan tidak bisa berfungsi sebagai kata kerja utama kalimat. Kalimat ini membutuhkan sebuah tindakan atau keadaan untuk menggambarkan apa yang dilakukan atau apa adanya batu safir.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 30: Form of the Verb, the sentence requires a past participle to form the passive voice, indicating that the sapphires were mined. The structure 'have been mined' correctly uses the past participle 'mined' after the auxiliary verb 'have' and implied 'been'. The sentence is stating a fact about sapphires that were found.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 30: Form of the Verb, kalimat ini membutuhkan past participle untuk membentuk passive voice, yang menunjukkan bahwa batu safir itu ditambang. Struktur 'have been mined' dengan benar menggunakan past participle 'mined' setelah kata kerja bantu 'have' dan 'been' yang tersirat. Kalimat ini menyatakan fakta tentang batu safir yang ditemukan.

questions.question: ~[Like]~(A) snakes, lizards can ~[be found]~(B) on all ~[others]~(C) continents ~[except]~(D) Antarctica.

reason.en: The word 'Like' is used correctly here as a preposition to draw a comparison. The sentence structure is sound with 'Like snakes' introducing a comparison.

reason.en: Based on Structure & Written Expression Skill 60: Commonly Confused Words (OTHER / ANOTHER / OTHERS), the word 'others' is used incorrectly. The correct word should be 'other' to function as an adjective.
